coreMetrics.name: Monthly Burn Rate
coreMetrics.description: Total cash outflow per month including salaries, tools, rent, and variable costs — the number you must know cold
coreMetrics.target: Decreasing as revenue scales, or stable during growth investment

coreMetrics.name: Runway Months
coreMetrics.description: Cash in bank divided by monthly burn rate — how many months you can survive at current spend

coreMetrics.name: Gross Margin
coreMetrics.description: Revenue minus cost of goods sold, divided by revenue — measures the efficiency of your core business

coreMetrics.name: MRR/ARR
coreMetrics.description: Monthly or Annual Recurring Revenue — the heartbeat of any subscription or retainer business

coreMetrics.name: Collections Ratio
coreMetrics.description: Percentage of invoiced revenue actually collected within payment terms — cash in hand, not on paper

weeklyRhythm.activity: Cash position check — review bank balances, incoming payments, and upcoming outflows for the week. Know your exact cash number.
Invoice review — check outstanding invoices, follow up on overdue payments, confirm next week's billing

monthlyRhythm.deliverable: Month-end P&L statement reviewed and annotated with variance explanations for any line item >10% off budget
Rolling 13-week cash flow forecast updated with actual data, upcoming contracts, and known expenses
Updated CAC, LTV, LTV:CAC ratio, and payback period calculations by customer segment
Variance analysis comparing budgeted vs actual spend by category, with action items for overruns

whatToDelegate.when: Immediately — daily transaction categorisation is not founder work
Once you have payment terms and follow-up templates established
whatToDelegate.toWhom: Office manager, VA, or accounts receivable contractor

whatToDelegate.when: Once your chart of accounts is set up and rules are documented
As soon as you have your first employee or contractor
